summary: Root cause analysis complete - Module resolution failure due to tsconfig path aliases not being resolved by tsx

rootCause.issue: Jarvis Core crashes on startup due to module resolution failure
rootCause.technicalDetails: Core service uses @shared/* path aliases defined in tsconfig.json, but tsx (TypeScript executor) does not automatically resolve these paths. When the code tries to import from '@shared/types/index.js', Node.js cannot find the module and the process exits.
evidence: tsx does not read tsconfig paths during execution
Process would exit with MODULE_NOT_FOUND error before binding to port 3010
rootCause.impact: Core never starts, Desktop cannot connect, all services show offline

fixApplied.description: Replaced all @shared/* path aliases with relative paths
fixApplied.secondaryFix: Added logs directory auto-creation to prevent logger crash

verification.step1: Core can now resolve all imports without tsconfig path aliases
verification.step2: Logger creates logs directory if missing
verification.step3: Core binds to port 3010 and responds to /health
verification.step4: Desktop at localhost:3011 can connect to Core at localhost:3010
verification.step6: Chat messages flow between Desktop and Core
